operator_scope.semantic_signature: candidate single-pass fusion for concrete KBX pointwise memory-bound tasks after correctness passes

valid_when: A legal concrete KBX pointwise candidate is hidden-correct.
The profile summary indicates memory_bound or launch_bound behavior and the expression is add, mul, GELU, fused add+GELU, fused mul-sub, or equivalent pointwise fusion.
invalid_when: The dominant operation is irregular gather/scatter, reduction, softmax, normalization, or matmul.
The candidate already performs a single low-level fused pass and profile indicates occupancy/register/arithmetic bottleneck.
preconditions: Apply semantic and legality capsules first; benchmark only legal hidden-correct candidates.

mechanism: A single custom kernel can reduce temporary tensors and launch count for pointwise chains, but concrete H20 benefit is workload-, dtype-, and layout-dependent.

instructions: Try one low-level kernel that computes the whole KBX pointwise expression in one pass with masked loads/stores.
Keep activation/arithmetic intermediates in registers and avoid materializing expanded/broadcast tensors.
Tune only a small block-size/num_warps set after correctness; do not hardcode visible shapes or public benchmark sizes.
Promote no speedup claim without repeated H20 benchmark and profile attribution.

expected_effect.correctness: Should preserve semantics only if sem_pointwise_kbx_entrypoint_broadcast_activation_v1 is followed.
expected_effect.performance: Candidate-only: may reduce memory passes or launch overhead; no H20 speedup is asserted.
expected_effect.search_efficiency: Gives A3 a bounded first optimization motif for concrete KBX pointwise tasks.
expected_effect.risk: May regress when stride/broadcast address math or activation register pressure dominates.

performance_requirements: Report p50 and p95 under the repository benchmark protocol.
Attach profile summary before any promotion.
validation.promotion_policy: Remain quarantine until repeated H20 runs or an ablation support the memory-pass or launch-count mechanism across more than one task/shape family.

known_failures: High-level PyTorch fallback can look fast but is illegal.
Fusion does not repair wrong broadcast or activation semantics.
Large blocks can lower occupancy or increase register pressure.
